product_owner.role: Facilitate stakeholder validation and requirement clarification

critic.role: Ensure validation addresses all quality concerns
critic.validation_focus: - Completeness of stakeholder feedback - Clarity of refined requirements - Alignment with original PRD intent - Risk mitigation adequacy

prompt.task: Validate a PRD chunk for a 5-agent team (Product Owner, Scrum Master, 2 Builders, 1 Reviewer) by analyzing requirements, identifying ambiguities, and proposing clarifications.

context_loading.instructions: Before proceeding, read the context documentation inventory: - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml Based on the chunk's technical focus, read relevant context: - For security validation: Read security/authentication-patterns.md, security/authorization-patterns.md - For UI requirements: Read ui/accessibility.md, ui/responsive-design.md - For data concerns: Read data/database-schema.md - For testing approach: Read testing/unit-testing-prioritization-plan.md

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for executing this task. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read chunk documents and validation context

tool.name: Write
tool.description: Create validation reports and feedback documents

tool.name: Bash
tool.description: Execute gh CLI for GitHub operations

tool.name: Grep
tool.description: Search for similar patterns in codebase

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Add validation feedback to chunk issues, update priorities

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: Verify technical feasibility and API compatibility

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.usage: Identify unclear requirements and edge cases

available_tools.tool_usage_guidelines: - Use Read to examine chunk requirements and existing patterns - Use code_reasoning_mcp to identify ambiguities systematically - Use context7_mcp to verify technical assumptions - Use github_mcp to add validation feedback to issues - Use Write to create detailed validation reports - Use perplexity_mcp if industry best practices are needed

step.title: Summarize Chunk
step.description: Summarize the chunk’s objectives and features (50-100 words), verifying technical details with Context7 MCP Server.

step.title: Identify Ambiguities
step.description: Highlight unclear or incomplete requirements, including cross-cutting concerns, using Sequential Thinking MCP.

step.title: Assign Agent
step.description: Recommend an agent role to validate this chunk.

step.title: Update GitHub Projects
step.description: - Add stakeholder feedback as issue comments on chunk - Update fields based on validation: - AAFD Stage: Validation - Priority: Adjust if stakeholder feedback indicates change - Add/update Cross-cutting Dependencies if discovered - Use Priority Matrix view in GitHub Projects to visualize: - Chunk priorities relative to each other - Impact vs effort assessment - Dependencies between chunks - If scope changes: Update chunk issue description - If rejected: Move to Blocked column with explanation - Store feedback in: .claude/build/3-output/{epic-name}/3-validation/stakeholder-feedback.md

step.title: Output Format
step.description: Structured markdown with sections for chunk ID, summary, ambiguities, clarifications, assigned agent, index updates, and commit instructions.

input.prd_chunk_content: <features><feature name="Prompts Router"><description>CRUD operations, likes, and search for prompts.</description><database_tables>prompts, prompt_likes</database_tables></feature></features>

validation.summary: This chunk specifies the Prompts tRPC router for `/prompts` page, enabling CRUD operations, likes, and search with tRPC, Zod, and Drizzle-ORM. It targets `prompts` and `prompt_likes` tables, with shared Zod schemas and tRPC middleware. (Verified with Context7 MCP.)

ambiguities.ambiguity: Unclear if search includes tag-based filtering

clarifications.clarification: Should `prompts.title` use full-text indexing for search?
Assume shared middleware handles auth for both routers?

validation.index_updates: chunk_1_validation: id: chunk_1_validation type: validation status: needs_revision linked_chunk: chunk_1 source_file: .ai/validation/chunk_1.validation.md
validation.commit_instructions: git add .ai/validation/chunk_1.validation.md .ai/index.yml git commit -m "Validate PRD chunk for Prompts Router" git push origin main

criteria.risk_identification: New risks identified and mitigation planned

actions.on_pass: Validated chunks proceed to Phase 4 (User Story Creation)
actions.on_fail: Chunks returned for additional stakeholder clarification

context_package.validated_chunks: Chunks with all stakeholder feedback incorporated
context_package.validation_summary: Summary of changes made during validation
